title: Stable release notes sync to the studiojin-home SyncWatcher page
tldr: After a stable GitHub Release is published, trigger a workflow in `kimjj81/studiojin-home` to import that release's notes into the `/syncwatcher/` product page, using a fine-grained PAT with only `Actions: write`.

title: Dry-run artifact reuse and pre-copy sync visibility
tldr: Store one backend-owned dry-run artifact per task, allow `Sync Now` to reuse that artifact after a light freshness check, and expose scan/compare progress in manual sync before copying starts.

title: Manual sync session UI and structured live sync events
tldr: Add task-scoped manual sync sessions in the renderer, emit structured sync live events with origin metadata, and keep background/watch sync from hijacking the manual result view.

title: Manual published-tag release recovery with explicit operator opt-in
tldr: Keep tag-push releases immutable by default, allow an explicit `workflow_dispatch` recovery path that reuses an already-published release only when the operator opts in, and require GitHub-generated release notes for every release creation or recovery run.

title: App Store channel split with StoreKit supporter state and sandbox bookmarks
tldr: Ship GitHub DMG and Mac App Store as separate channels, keep optional supporter purchases provider-specific, submit the App Store build locally instead of through GitHub release automation, use best-effort App Store update notices, and persist user-selected path access with security-scoped bookmarks.

title: In-process recurring schedule engine and file-backed history
tldr: Store recurring schedules inside each sync task, execute them only while the running app process is alive, and persist per-schedule run history plus per-run detail entries as separate files under app support state.

title: Runtime watch activation re-enqueues initial sync
tldr: After the first runtime bootstrap, any watch task that newly activates or restarts must enqueue one initial sync so existing diffs are not stranded until the next filesystem event.

title: Producer completion gate for safe downstream watch sync
tldr: Allow acyclic watch chains, but delay downstream watch sync until every overlapping writer succeeds and a short settle window has elapsed.

title: Watch chain cycle gating and downstream settle window
tldr: Allow acyclic watch chains, reject only true watch cycles, and delay downstream watch sync until upstream sync completes plus a short settle window.

title: ARM-first release orchestration with draft reuse and published-tag fail-fast
tldr: Create or reuse one draft release per tag, let the ARM build author the canonical updater metadata first, append x64 assets afterward, and fail fast when a tag already has a published release; superseded by ADR-20260404-0022-REL for explicit manual published-tag recovery.

title: Lemon Squeezy handles supporter licensing while GitHub Releases remain the distribution origin for v1
tldr: Use Lemon Squeezy for optional supporter purchases and license-key lifecycle, but keep GitHub Releases as the source of install files and Tauri updater metadata in v1.

title: SyncTask source identity snapshot and guided UUID update
tldr: Keep UUID source tokens as the runtime contract, persist extra removable-media identity hints beside each task, and offer conservative user-approved UUID update recommendations when a stored UUID no longer resolves.

title: Live dry-run session reentry and terminal immutability
tldr: Treat live dry-run sessions as task-scoped UI state, keep terminal sessions immutable against late events, and expose MCP-origin dry-run sessions through task-button reentry instead of auto-navigation.

title: Target path preflight and missing-target policy
tldr: Classify missing target paths before dry-run and sync, fail fast for unmounted target volumes, and treat missing mounted subdirectories as create-on-sync with explicit user-facing warnings.

title: Exclusion defaults consolidation into a program set
tldr: Replace per-ecosystem built-in exclusion defaults with one `program` built-in set, automatically migrate legacy built-in IDs in stored exclusion sets and tasks, and remove project-authored files like `.gitignore` and `.env` from built-in defaults.

title: Launch-at-login autostart with hidden startup
tldr: Use Tauri's autostart plugin on macOS, keep login-item state as the OS-owned source of truth, and start hidden only when both the autostart argument and OS login-item state confirm a real login launch.

title: Enabled-only MCP relay for a running-app control plane
tldr: Expose a local stdio MCP relay mode on the main SyncWatcher executable only when the user explicitly enables MCP in SyncWatcher settings, and route all MCP actions through the running app backend over a Unix socket with backend-owned canonical config.

title: Release SBOMs, checksum manifests, stable cosign attestation, and separate local verification
tldr: Generate SBOMs and checksum manifests for every tagged release, force keyless cosign attestations for stable tags, keep prerelease attestation opt-in via a repo variable, and keep local cosign verification separate from the latest installer.

title: watchMode zero-copy auto-unmount deferred confirmation
tldr: When watch sync copies zero files, replace immediate auto-unmount with notification + deferred in-app confirmation, and unmount only after explicit approval.

title: Exclusion template expansion and exclusion-pattern limit increase
tldr: Expand default exclusion templates across major ecosystems, merge missing defaults for existing users once, and raise exclusion-pattern limit from 100 to 300 with runtime deduplication.

title: Target-newer conflict hash-equivalence exception
tldr: When target mtime is newer, classify as conflict only if content differs; if source and target are hash-identical, treat as no-op.

title: Target-newer conflict deferred review in dedicated window
tldr: When target files are newer than source, skip auto-copy and defer handling to an explicit review session in a separate conflict-review window.

title: Runtime watch-mode initial sync queue and concurrency limit
tldr: Runtime watch sync uses a bounded queue (max parallelism 2), coalesces watch events during active sync into one replay, and serializes overlapping runtime config applies.

title: Sync task target conflict policy, UUID runtime validation, and orphan cleanup workflow
tldr: Remove automatic target-side deletion, enforce source/target overlap constraints at runtime (including UUID sources), and replace deletion with explicit orphan scan and confirmed manual cleanup.

title: App lifecycle (quit vs background)
tldr: Keep window-close behavior stable under `closeAction`, add source-aware close events, and apply Cmd+Q-only confirmation policies.

title: Dashboard volume source of truth
tldr: Stop scanning `/Volumes`; use `getmntinfo_r_np` as source of truth, and apply a single app-wide capacity unit policy (default binary/IEC).
